Most likely that they 
only consider their own context when they try to prove 
something. This is fine. Evaluate the usefulness of their 
point of view." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="104.08" width="508.71" x="1216.54" y="5229.53" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="80" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#FFD5E4;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=20;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="Take challenging tasks in order to develop your skills and 
competencies, even when there is not enough 
knowledge/experience (skills will develop much faster 
through such tasks). The main thing is to distinguish a 
really useful complex task that will help you to boost your 
skills from a dumb task that requires a scapegoat." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="177.8" width="564.28" x="1216.54" y="5593.21" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="81" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#FFD5E4;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=20;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="You should be responsible for your professional development. Don't wait 
for the team lead to decide for you what to study. Do not forget that blind 
adherence to someone else's plan will sharpen your skills according to 
requirements of a particular company. It is better to discuss and build in 
your interests into the plan. If this is not done, there is a high risk of getting 
irrelevant skills, which will hit career opportunities. But don't overdo it: don't 
study absolutely everything or don't passively wait until a development plan 
will be created for you." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="248.83" width="706.53" x="982.54" y="5869.51" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="82" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#FFD5E4;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=20;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="Self-organization" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60.92" width="164.09" x="2104.98" y="6281.51" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="83" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#FFD5E4;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=20;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="The ability to provide the amount of information that is 
necessary to communicate to people with different 
backgrounds." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="88.12" width="495.37" x="20" y="6469.79" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="84" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#FFD5E4;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=20;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="Conflict solving" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="56.12" width="148.5" x="1207.22" y="6578.33" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="85" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#FFD5E4;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=20;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="If you read this it means you already understand why it is so

style="rounded=1;html=0;fillColor=#FFE5B9;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=100;fontColor=#000000;fontStyle=1;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="C++ Developer Roadmap" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="328.98" width="4118.12" x="615.9" y="20" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="398" parent="1" style="rounded=1;html=0;fillColor=#FFE5B9;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=50;fontColor=#000000;fontStyle=1;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="About:" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="108.72" width="706.5" x="604.68" y="700.13" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="399" parent="1" style="rounded=1;html=0;fillColor=#FFF5EB;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=24;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="C ++ is still one of the most popular development languages. There is quite a number of people that want to start learning it 
and become a C++ developer. They face questions like: "Where should I start? What and in what order should I study? What 
books should I read?"

We tried to answer these questions in the presented roadmap. The map focuses on general competencies and skills that 
could be found in most projects. It is designed to help those who are just starting their education or have little experience. 
You can create a more productive learning plan without being distracted by side information if you study the set of listed 
materials. It will help you to master C++ at the level that is used in many commercial projects." vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="328.98" width="1343.25" x="604.68" y="860.2" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="400" parent="1" style="rounded=1;html=0;fillColor=#FFE5B9;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=50;fontColor=#000000;fontStyle=1;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="How to use:" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="108.72" width="717.73" x="604.68" y="1780.93" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="401" parent="1" style="rounded=1;html=0;fillColor=#FFF5EB;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=24;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="If you have any feedback or remarks don't hesitate to contact us. 
We really appreciate it. :)

To contact us choose one of the following Github approaches:
- For any remarks related to repository content - go to Pull 
Requests and submit a new one" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="328.98" width="706.96" x="1371.04" y="1941.01" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="402" parent="1" style="rounded=1;html=0;fillColor=#FFE5B9;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=50;fontColor=#000000;fontStyle=1;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="Feedback:" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="108.72" width="706.96" x="1371.04" y="1780.93" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="403" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#CCEEFF;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=24;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="Optional" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40.51" width="103.39" x="912.77" y="1919.65" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="404" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#96BB7C;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=24;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="Junior" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40.51" width="79.37" x="924.78" y="2070" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="405" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#FAD586;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=24;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="Middle" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40.51" width="84.7" x="922.11" y="2020.49" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="406" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#BBCCEE;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=24;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="Senior" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40.51" width="83.38" x="922.77" y="1970.97" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="407" parent="1" style="rounded=0;html=0;fillColor=#FAD586;strokeColor=#000000;strokeWidth=1;fontSize=16;fontColor=#000000;verticalAlign=middle;align=center;" value="The middle developer has the

g.text: At first, some of the skills may seem impossible to
develop. However, we think differently. It is possible to
work on each of the skills. Everything depends on your
desire and internal blocks. Everything is individual, and
it's up to you to decide. :)

g.text: Calmly react to what people say. Most likely that they
only consider their own context when they try to prove
something. This is fine. Evaluate the usefulness of their

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 213, 228), rgb(73, 37, 50)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237));

g.text: Take challenging tasks in order to develop your skills and
competencies, even when there is not enough
through such tasks). The main thing is to distinguish a
really useful complex task that will help you to boost your
skills from a dumb task that requires a scapegoat.

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 213, 228), rgb(73, 37, 50)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237));

g.text: You should be responsible for your professional development. Don't wait
for the team lead to decide for you what to study. Do not forget that blind
adherence to someone else's plan will sharpen your skills according to
requirements of a particular company. It is better to discuss and build in
your interests into the plan. If this is not done, there is a high risk of getting
irrelevant skills, which will hit career opportunities. But don't overdo it: don't
study absolutely everything or don't passively wait until a development plan

g.text: If you read this it means you already understand why it is so
Usually, it is required at least to read because you would need to
understand technical documentation. But as you move up the career
ladder, you will need to speak and write more confidently in English.

competitive, as well as being able to adapt to them.

g.text: Experienced developers should develop management skills and business
communication skills to complete tasks efficiently. It is easier to do if you
have developed emotional intelligence. For a junior developer, the skill is
also useful to better interact with colleagues.

g.text: C ++ is still one of the most popular development languages. There is quite a number of people that want to start learning it
and become a C++ developer. They face questions like: "Where should I start? What and in what order should I study? What
We tried to answer these questions in the presented roadmap. The map focuses on general competencies and skills that
could be found in most projects. It is designed to help those who are just starting their education or have little experience.
You can create a more productive learning plan without being distracted by side information if you study the set of listed
materials. It will help you to master C++ at the level that is used in many commercial projects.
